title: Anywhere Door Trip Planner
description: A cheerful routing tool for surprise visits, class field trips, and last-minute dorayaki runs across town.

title: Take-copter Flight School
description: A beginner-friendly aerial training program with soft landing drills, breeze checks, and tiny pilot badges.

title: Time Machine Debugger
description: A timeline troubleshooting dashboard for spotting cause-and-effect tangles before they turn into future homework disasters.

title: Memory Bread Study Cafe
description: A cozy exam-prep concept that pairs textbook toast with study timers, snack breaks, and slightly nervous confidence.

title: Small Light Tiny Museum
description: A miniature exhibition kit for shrinking big ideas into pocket-sized displays that fit on Nobita's desk.

title: Big Light Parade Kit
description: A playful scaling system for making tiny decorations, toy floats, and shy ideas big enough for a neighborhood parade.

title: Translation Jelly Picnic
description: A language-sharing picnic setup where everyone can swap stories, recipes, and jokes without getting stuck on vocabulary.

title: Pocket Inventory Sorter
description: A tidy catalog for Doraemon's four-dimensional pocket, designed to find the right gadget before the panic music starts.

title: Dorayaki Snack Forecast
description: A sweet little tracker that predicts snack shortages, favorite fillings, and the best time to restock the pantry.

title: Homework Courage Console
description: A supportive dashboard that breaks scary assignments into tiny quests with encouragement between each checkpoint.

title: Lost Button Finder
description: A small domestic search system for runaway buttons, missing pencils, and other objects hiding under impossible furniture.

title: Rainy Day Cloud Painter
description: A gentle weather-mood project that turns gloomy afternoons into tiny sky sketches and indoor adventure plans.
